filters.hidden_core: English Heritage plaque AND absent from OSM tourism layer within 50m AND <=3 tourism POIs within 500m AND within 800m of a TfL station
filters.hidden_quiet: hidden_core AND green-space centroid within 400m AND major road farther than 100m

bloomsbury_backstreets.label: Bloomsbury Backstreets
bloomsbury_backstreets.description: Writers, artists and thinkers in Camden and Westminster away from the main tourist trail.

west_london_creatives.label: West London Creatives
west_london_creatives.description: Overlooked arts and design plaques across Chelsea, Kensington and riverside streets.

civic_voices.label: Civic Voices
civic_voices.description: Politicians, campaigners and scientists whose plaques sit in accessible but overlooked neighbourhoods.

garden_suburb_retreats.label: Garden Suburb Retreats
garden_suburb_retreats.description: Quieter residential plaques near parks and side streets across outer London.

literary_lives_walk.label: Literary Lives Walk
literary_lives_walk.description: Novelists, poets and editors clustered into a tighter route for visitors who want a theme-led walk.

riverside_quiet_edge.label: Riverside Quiet Edge
riverside_quiet_edge.description: Plaques close to river or canal settings where cultural memory meets calmer waterfront detours.

parkside_pause.label: Parkside Pause
parkside_pause.description: Shorter quiet-hidden route favouring plaques near parks, commons and greener side streets.

step_free_starter.label: Step-Free Starter
step_free_starter.description: A more accessible starter route using plaques near lift-served TfL stations and shorter walks.
step_free_starter.focus: Shorter approach and easier station access

properties.name: ESSEX STREET
properties.address: Essex Hall, Essex Street, Strand, London, WC2, City of Westminster
properties.summary: Blue Plaque commemorating the lawyers who lived at Essex Street at 122 Regent's Park Road, Primrose Hill, London NW1 8XL, London Borough of Camden.
properties.official_category_text: Architecture and Building, Historical Sites, Journalism and Publishing, Literature, Politics and Administration
properties.inscription: ESSEX STREET was laid out in the grounds of Essex House by NICHOLAS BARBON in 1675 Among many famous lawyers who lived here were Sir ORLANDO BRIDGEMAN c.1606-1674 Lord Keeper HENRY FIELDING 1707-1754 Novelist and BRASS CROSBY 1725-1793 Lord Mayor of London JAMES SAVAGE 1779-1852 Architect had his office here. PRINCE CHARLES EDWARD STUART stayed at a house in the street in 1750. Rev. THEOPHILUS LINDSEY 1723-1808 Unitarian Minister founded Essex Street Chapel here in 1774. Dr. SAMUEL JOHNSON established an evening club at the Essex Head in 1783

properties.name: WOMEN’S FREEDOM LEAGUE
properties.address: 1 Robert Street, London, WC2N 6RL, City of Westminster
properties.summary: The Women’s Freedom League was a suffragist and equal rights campaigning organisation that worked on the principles of passive resistance and non-violence. Formed after a split in the Women’s Social and Political Union, it had its former headquarters at 1 Robert Street, Strand, which is marked with an English Heritage blue plaque.
properties.inscription: The WOMEN’S FREEDOM LEAGUE campaigned for women’s equality from here 1908–1915
